text: Binance Research said four BTC on-chain indicators suggest available sell-side supply may be tightening. Nearly 60% of Bitcoin supply has not moved for over a year, while exchange balances have fallen from 17.6% around the COVID-era peak to 15.0%, implying that roughly 500k BTC have left exchanges. The report also noted that SLRV remains near historical lows, suggesting subdued speculation, while BTC STH MVRV has moved back above 1.0 after staying below that level for much of the period since November 2024, indicating that short-term holders are rebuilding unrealized profits. — link

text: Highlight Clip CZ: We Still Invest 70%–80% of Our Capital in Blockchain In a May 7 speech at Consensus Miami 2026, Binance founder Changpeng Zhao (CZ) said he and Yzi Labs still allocate 70% to 80% of their capital to blockchain, as it remains their core area of expertise. He said AI agents will create significant future demand for payments, and blockchain is the more native and natural payment tool for them. CZ also said exchanges should prepare for AI agents: instead of manually reading charts and entering prices and quantities, users should be able to use natural language to let AI agents review portfolios and execute trades. — link

text: The Agent's Legal Body: How AI Agents Get the Right to Contract AI agents can already act, negotiate, and manage money, but they can't legally own, contract, or be sued, making every agent transaction collapse back to a human signature and creating the real bottleneck for agentic commerce. > This piece argues that the solution already exists in a 2014 legal insight called the "Bayern Mechanism", wrapping an AI agent in a zero-member LLC that lets it own assets, sign contracts, and bear liability. Source:

text: How has your experience with alt.fun been so far? I've been trying it for a few days now. The first day was a disaster, I couldn't even load the website or buy/sell tokens. By the time I got home, everything had already gone to dust lol So I cut a lot of losses on day one. I bought $ALT at a $700K MC, $STONK at a $300K MC, $MIM at a $20K MC, and a bunch of tokens that eventually did 10x-30x (but of course I couldn't hold). I got spooked by the first day experience, so every time I went out, I sold everything... Then I made profit again, jeeted for quick flips, and round-tripped into new shiny pre-bonded tokens What I've learned so far if you want to make money on alt.fun: - $ALT will probably remain the safest choice as long as the platform survives, if u want to buy imo have to wait for dips. - Split your capital into different buckets: a larger allocation for top-tier plays like $ALT or $STONKS, a medium allocation for mid-tier tokens with high trading volume, and only 2–5% for pre-bonded tokens (preferably ones with high volume and decent holder distribution). - Most of the volume comes from the Chinese community, so follow Chinese KOLs and traders. Some people I follow (mainly for insights): And join the group: (there's also a Chinese channel) I still believe $ALT has room to go higher. It gives me the same feeling as the early HyperEVM trenches days with hfun. Shoutout to the team for bringing volume back to HyperEVM. Right now I'm in $ALT, $STONKS, $TOP, $chameleon (and some others that I top-blasted into... )

text: "Some people dream of having a swimming pool at home, while those who have one hardly ever use it. Those who have lost a loved one feel a profound sense of loss, while others often complain about their living relatives. Those without a partner long for one, while those who have one often don't appreciate it. The hungry would give anything for a meal, while the satiated complain about the taste of their food. Those without a car dream of owning one, while those who have a car are always looking for a better one." The key to happiness is gratitude: truly seeing and appreciating what we already have, and understanding that somewhere, someone would give anything for what we take for granted.
author: admin#1742094092

text: Jump Crypto's Firedancer Quietly Enters Production on Solana Mainnet Firedancer, a Solana validator client developed by Jump Crypto, the crypto arm of high-frequency trading firm Jump Trading, is now live on Solana mainnet and producing blocks. Founding engineer Ritchie Patel said the client has processed tens of millions of transactions in production, but the team will continue a gradual rollout and does not want validators switching at scale before full security audits are completed. The team also recently completed a public audit contest with a $1 million bug bounty pool. — link
author: admin#1534592031

text: Washington Post: More Than One-Fifth of Trump Administration Officials Hold Crypto According to The Washington Post, nearly 70 officials and nominees in the Trump administration disclosed holdings in cryptocurrencies or investments in blockchain and digital asset companies, accounting for over one-fifth of nearly 300 senior appointees reviewed. Total disclosed crypto-related holdings amounted to at least $193 million. President Donald Trump alone reported at least $51 million in digital assets, while Vice President JD Vance and multiple Cabinet members also disclosed bitcoin or related crypto investments. The report noted that no Biden Cabinet officials reported digital currency holdings, making the Trump administration the most crypto-exposed U.S. administration to date. — link

text: Harvard Endowment Exits Ethereum ETF and Cuts IBIT Stake by 43% Q1 2026 SEC 13F filings show that Harvard's endowment cut its IBIT position by about 43% to 3,044,612 shares, worth roughly $117 million, after already reducing the stake by 21% in Q4. It also fully exited its $86.8 million position in BlackRock's spot Ethereum ETF. Meanwhile, Abu Dhabi sovereign wealth fund Mubadala increased its IBIT holdings to 14,721,917 shares, worth about $566 million, up from 12,702,323 shares at the end of 2025. — link

text: Highlight Clip Michael Saylor: Stretch Is The Biggest Preffered Stock in The World On May 6, 2026, at Consensus 2026 Miami, Michael Saylor announced that Stretch (STRC) is now the world's largest and most liquid preferred stock, with $8.5 billion in AUM. Saylor noted that while Bitcoin dropped 37% over the past six months, STRC credit investors held par and earned a 64% dividend yield. — link
